description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone wall forming the main body, topped by a pitched roof made of weathered wooden shingles. The well sits on a round, slightly raised base that appears to be covered in moss or grass, suggesting outdoor placement. The roof is supported by two sturdy wooden posts, and a horizontal wooden beam runs across the top, supporting a simple hand crank mechanism on one side. The interior of the well is open, revealing a dark void where water would be drawn. The overall appearance is rustic, with visible texture on the stone and wood, and the structure is robust and functional, designed to provide access to groundwater in a communal or public setting.
description: A rustic stone water well with a pitched wooden roof, hand crank, and circular stone wall, set on a mossy base. The structure is robust and weathered, suitable for outdoor urban or park environments.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stone wall, pitched roof, and hand crank mechanism on the right side.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the wooden roof supports and the open back of the well, with no crank visible.
description_view_2: Similar to the front, this view shows the stone wall and roof, with the hand crank on the left side.
description_view_3: Rear angled view focusing on the wooden supports and the open structure of the well.
attribute_well_cover_design: No cover; the well opening is fully exposed beneath the pitched roof.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A simple hand crank mechanism is present on the side, made of wood and metal; no bucket or pulley is visible.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately waist-high surrounds the well; a pitched wooden roof provides overhead protection.
